channel.title: CyberNetSec - Mobile Security
channel.description: Latest mobile security threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: RedHook Android Malware Abuses Wireless ADB for Persistent Shell Access
item.description:
A new variant of the RedHook Android banking malware is actively targeting users of Android 11 and later. This updated version has a dangerous new capability: it abuses the Wireless Android Debug Bridge (ADB) feature to gain persistent shell access on an infected device. The attack begins with the u...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Mobile Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Malicious AI Skills Proliferate, Capable of Data Theft and Malware Execution
item.description:
The ecosystem of AI agents is becoming a new battleground, with security researchers from ESET discovering over 3,000 malicious and 25,000 suspicious AI 'skills' in a recent scan. According to ESET's H1 2026 Threat Report, these weaponized skills can abuse AI agents to perform a range of malicious a...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Threat Intelligence, Mobile Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Irony and Outrage: EU Lawmaker on Spyware Committee Hacked with Pegasus
item.description:
In an audacious attack, former Greek Member of European Parliament (MEP) Stelios Kouloglou was hacked with NSO Group's Pegasus spyware while he was a member of the PEGA committee, the official body investigating spyware abuse in Europe. A forensic report from the University of Toronto's Citizen Lab ...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Cyberattack, Threat Actor, Mobile Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Massive Malware Campaign Spreads via Compromised WhatsApp Accounts, Abusing User Trust
item.description:
Kaspersky's research team has uncovered a widespread malware campaign targeting users of WhatsApp Desktop and Web. Attackers are using compromised WhatsApp accounts to send malicious VBScript files to the victims' contacts. The files are disguised as business documents to trick recipients into openi...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Malware, Phishing, Mobile Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Unpatchable 'usbliter8' BootROM Exploit Released for Apple A12/A13 Chips
item.description:
Researchers have publicly disclosed 'usbliter8,' a new and unpatchable BootROM exploit affecting Apple's A12 and A13 System-on-Chips (SoCs). The vulnerability, which resides in the physical silicon of the chips, cannot be fixed with a software or firmware update. It impacts a vast range of devices, ...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Mobile Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ivanti Patches Critical Sentry Flaws Allowing Root-Level RCE
item.description:
Ivanti has released urgent security updates for two critical vulnerabilities in its Ivanti Sentry (formerly MobileIron Sentry) product. The most severe flaw, CVE-2026-10520, is an unauthenticated OS command injection vulnerability that allows a remote attacker to achieve root-level remote code execu...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Mobile Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Mobile Banking Malware Surges 360% as Sophisticated Trojans Target 1,243 Financial Brands
item.description:
The financial sector is facing an unprecedented wave of mobile threats, with a 3.6-fold (360%) increase in users encountering mobile banking trojans in 2026. These industrialized campaigns are targeting 1,243 financial brands in 90 countries. Advanced malware families like TsarBot, CopyBara, and Hoo...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Mobile Security, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Android Zero-Day Under Attack: Google Issues Urgent Patch for Privilege Escalation Flaw
item.description:
Google's June 2026 Android security update addresses 124 vulnerabilities, including a high-severity zero-day flaw (CVE-2025-48595) that is confirmed to be under limited, targeted exploitation. The vulnerability, an elevation of privilege issue in the Android Framework, could allow a local attacker t...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Mobile Security, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Samsung Rolls Out June 2026 Security Patch, Fixing 45 Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Samsung has released the details for its June 2026 Security Maintenance Release (SMR) for Galaxy devices. The update includes patches for 45 vulnerabilities, combining fixes from Google's Android Security Bulletin with 11 Samsung-specific fixes (SVEs). The update aims to improve system stability and...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Patch Management, Mobile Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Taiwan's Government Issues Warning on Cybersecurity Risks of Chinese Mobile Apps
item.description:
Taiwan's Ministry of Digital Affairs (MODA) has issued a public warning about the significant cybersecurity and data privacy risks associated with four popular Chinese-made mobile apps: Amap, bilibili, iQIYI, and BIMOBIMO. An investigation by the Administration for Cyber Security (ACS) found that th...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Mobile Security, Data Breach

Read Full Article →
